title: Buxar Fort
tagline: Buxar Fort — heritage city of Bihar

overview.short: Buxar Fort is a fort located in Buxar, Bihar, India.
overview.description: Buxar Fort is a fort located in Buxar, Bihar, India. Buxar is a city in the state of Bihar in the eastern part of India bordering eastern Uttar Pradesh. It is the headquarters of Buxar District. The fort was established by King Rudra Deo in 1054 .

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Buxar
topPlaces.description: Buxar is a nagar parishad city in the state of Bihar, India, sharing border with Balia and Ghazipur district of Uttar Pradesh. It is the headquarters of the eponymous Buxar district, as well as the headquarters of the community development block of Buxar, which also contains the census town of Sarimpur along with 132 rural villages.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Buxar
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Buxar was fought between 22 and 23 October 1764, between the forces of the British East India Company, under the command of Major Hector Munro, against the combined armies of Shah Alam II, the Emperor of the Mughal Empire; Mir Qasim, the Nawab of Bengal; Balwant Singh, the Maharaja of the Benares State; Shuja-ud-daula, the Nawab of Awadh. The battle was fought at Buxar, a "strong fortified town" within the territory of Bihar, located on the banks of the Ganges river about 130 kilometres (81 mi) west of Patna; it was a challenging victory for the British East India Company.

topPlaces.name: Diocese of Buxar
topPlaces.description: The Roman Catholic Diocese of Buxar (Latin: Dioecesis Buxarensis) in India was created on December 12, 2005. It is a suffragan diocese of the Archdiocese of Patna.

topPlaces.name: Bharouli Khas, Ballia
topPlaces.description: Bharouli Khas is a village in Ballia district, Uttar Pradesh, India.

topPlaces.name: Kanuan
topPlaces.description: Kanuan Also known as kanuwan is a village located in Mohammadabad Assembly constituency tehsil of Ghazipur , Uttar Pradesh. It is situated 45 km from Ghazipur district.

topPlaces.name: Itarhi, Buxar
topPlaces.description: Itarhi (Hindi: इटाढ़ी) is a Nagar Panchayat and corresponding community development block in Buxar district of Bihar, India. According to the 2011 Census of India, its population is 10,275, in 1,711 households, while the total block population is 170,629, in 25,557 households.

topPlaces.name: Sukhdehri
topPlaces.description: Sukhdehri Kalan is a village located in Mohammadabad tehsil of Ghazipur district, Uttar Pradesh. It has total 110 families residing.

items.note: Buxar is a nagar parishad city in the state of Bihar, India, sharing border with Balia and Ghazipur district of Uttar Pradesh. It is the headquarters of the eponymous Buxar district, as well as the headquarters of the community development block of Buxar, which also contains the census town of Sarimpur along with 132 rural villages.

The Battle of Buxar was fought between 22 and 23 October 1764, between the forces of the British East India Company, under the command of Major Hector Munro, against the combined armies of Shah Alam II, the Emperor of the Mughal Empire; Mir Qasim, the Nawab of Bengal; Balwant Singh, the Maharaja of the Benares State; Shuja-ud-daula, the Nawab of Awadh. The battle was fought at Buxar, a "strong fortified town" within the territory of Bihar, located on the banks of the Ganges river about 130 kilometres (81 mi) west of Patna; it was a challenging victory for the British East India Company.
The Roman Catholic Diocese of Buxar (Latin: Dioecesis Buxarensis) in India was created on December 12, 2005. It is a suffragan diocese of the Archdiocese of Patna.
Bharouli Khas is a village in Ballia district, Uttar Pradesh, India.
Kanuan Also known as kanuwan is a village located in Mohammadabad Assembly constituency tehsil of Ghazipur , Uttar Pradesh. It is situated 45 km from Ghazipur district.

Itarhi (Hindi: इटाढ़ी) is a Nagar Panchayat and corresponding community development block in Buxar district of Bihar, India. According to the 2011 Census of India, its population is 10,275, in 1,711 households, while the total block population is 170,629, in 25,557 households.
Sukhdehri Kalan is a village located in Mohammadabad tehsil of Ghazipur district, Uttar Pradesh. It has total 110 families residing.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Buxar Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Buxar Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Buxar Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jay Prakash Narayan Airport, Patna (111 km) and the nearest railway station is Varanasi Junction (103 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Buxar Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Buxar Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Buxar Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Buxar Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Buxar Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Buxar, Battle of Buxar, Diocese of Buxar, Bharouli Khas, Ballia, Kanuan.
